title: Where nine of the best remaining NFL free agents should sign: We found perfect landing spots
content: Where should Jauan Jennings, Joey Bosa and Rasheed Walker sign? Which roster needs Kirk Cousins? Let's find new homes for nine players still looking for one.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: Big 12 to swap LED glass court for hardwood
content: The Big 12 will transition back to a traditional hardwood court for the remainder of its conference tournament at T-Mobile Center after the innovative LED glass court it was using led to some slipping and grip issues for players.

title: Spoelstra sorry to 'absolutely no one' for Bam's 83
content: Heat coach Erik Spoelstra brushed aside critics of Bam Adebayo's 83-point game, saying, "You have to go for it, and that was just thrilling."

title: Luka drops 51 as Lakers explode in LeBron's return
content: Buoyed by LeBron James' return, Luka Doncic scored 51 points on a night in which the Lakers' starting five totaled 137 points, the most by a starting lineup in a regulation game since the 1970-71 season.

title: Estimated $3.3B in NCAA tourney bets, AGA says
content: Approximately $3.3 billion is expected to be bet on the NCAA men's and women's basketball tournaments with U.S. sportsbooks, a slight year-over-year increase, according to a gaming association's estimates.

title: Celts' Brown calls out NBA's 'foul baiting' trend
content: The Celtics' Jaylen Brown expressed frustration with what he called the league's trend of rewarding players for "foul baiting," saying, "I just don't think it's basketball."

title: Murray joins Vikings, 'elevating' their QB room
content: A day after the Cardinals officially released him, Kyler Murray signed a one-year contract with the Vikings and will join former first-round pick J.J. McCarthy in the QB room in Minnesota.

title: SGA eclipses Wilt for NBA's longest 20-point streak
content: Thunder star Shai Gilgeous-Alexander made history Thursday night against the Celtics, breaking Wilt Chamberlain's NBA record with his 127th consecutive regular-season game with at least 20 points.

title: Leafs star Matthews (knee) injured in 'dirty play'
content: Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews left Thursday night against Anaheim after a knee-on-knee hit from Ducks defenseman Radko Gudas.

title: SGA breaks Wilt's record: The 20 wildest facts of Thunder star's 127 games of dominance
content: With Gilgeous-Alexander holding the record for most consecutive 20-point games, Kram breaks down the stats.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Clemson graduates to 'lock' with win over North Carolina
content: The Tigers held on to defeat the Tar Heels in the ACC tournament, leaving room for little doubt come Sunday.

title: Women's Bracketology: Champ Week upsets ripple through the field
content: Dreams are being realized and crushed during Champ Week. Stephen F. Austin pulled the latest upset to clinch an auto-bid.

title: Women's college basketball awards and All-Americans: UConn's Sarah Strong leads way
content: Mikayla Blakes made sure POY wasn't a one-player race, and Aaliyah Chavez had company in the FOY talk.

title: What Kyler Murray signing means for Vikings, J.J. McCarthy -- and Justin Jefferson's fantasy value
content: Will Murray resurrect his career and boost the Vikings' offense after being released by the Cardinals?

title: Is the USA No. 1? Can anyone beat D.R.? Reranking the remaining World Baseball Classic teams
content: We're down to eight. Here's what we've learned -- and who has impressed us most -- as we enter the quarterfinals.

title: How Dominican Republic sluggers are lighting up WBC
content: Bat flips, home run trots and ... a plantain dumbbell? One team might be having more fun than any other.

title: NHL playoff watch: Projections for the Oilers' postseason path
content: Here's how it's shaping up for Edmonton. Plus, full Stanley Cup playoff projections, draft lottery standings and more.

title: Weekend predictions: Can Spurs stop record losing streak at Liverpool?
content: It's Friday, and another full weekend of action awaits across the soccer calendar as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

title: Prediction markets' unpredictable future amid conflicting court rulings
content: Regulatory authority is at the center of growing battle in courts, Congress, statehouses and the CFTC.

title: NBA draft intel and latest buzz: Peterson or Dybantsa at No. 1? What's Boozer's case?
content: Here's what league insiders are saying about Darryn Peterson, AJ Dybantsa and more top NBA draft prospects.

title: Can the Knicks get Towns, Brunson on track? Five issues they need to solve -- fast
content: The Knicks are 42-25 and third in the Eastern Conference, but they have a few problems to solve if they want to make a deep playoff run.

title: Why Miami is facing its 'most challenging' offseason yet of the Cristobal era
content: Can Miami reach college football's mountaintop again? Mario Cristobal has the Canes ready to climb again.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: Talks in 'right direction' as marathon meeting continues
content: League personnel and WNBPA staff have met for three consecutive days but have not yet reached a new collective bargaining agreement.

title: Olympic champ Alysa Liu receives key to city in hometown rally
content: The Olympic figure skating champion received a key to her hometown during a rally that included performances from Bay Area artists.

title: Tension builds ahead of NFLPA leadership vote
content: NFL Players Association representatives are set to vote on a successor to disgraced former leader Lloyd Howell.

title: Betting on the Oscars: From 'Marty Supreme' to 'KPop Demon Hunters'
content: Do you think you know who's winning at the Oscars? See what the sportsbooks say, then make your final picks.

title: Ravens tied for second-shortest Super Bowl odds after whirlwind week
content: Every NFL team's team's win total, as well as odds to win their division, the conference and Super Bowl LXI.

title: Haney vs. Garcia 2? Mayweather vs. Tyson still happening?
content: Andreas Hale answers some key questions to bring clarity to the most intriguing storylines in boxing today.

title: NWSL 2026 mega-preview: Key moves, questions and predictions for all 16 teams
content: The NWSL is back! Let's predict how each team will finish by assessing what's ahead for Rodman, Lavelle and the other stars.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Agent of PSG's Ballon d'Or Dembélé meets with Man City
content: Ballon d'Or holder Ousmane Dembélé could be lined up for a future move from Paris Saint-Germain to Manchester City. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Guide to NFL offseason signings: Can Geno Smith rebound from 2025 in New York?
content: Your one-stop shop for fantasy football reaction to all the key player movement via free agency and trades.

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: Game Of Drones: Ukraine's Sting Answer For US' Shahed Problem
content: Iran Israel War: The Iranian-designed Shahed-136 drone has become one of the most recognisable weapons of the modern battlefield.

title: Mumbai Engineer Dies In Ship Attack Near Iraq, Daughter Recounts Final Call
content: An Indian marine engineer from Mumbai was killed after an attack on an oil tanker near Iraq, leaving his family devastated and demanding answers.

title: 2 Indians Killed, 10 Injured In Drone Strike In Oman
content: The government has said it is closely monitoring the safety of Indians across the Gulf region where nearly one crore Indian nationals live and work.

title: Houthis Signal Readiness But Have Not Yet Joined the US Iran Israel War
content: Iran's Shi'ite allies in Lebanon and Iraq have joined the war in the region unleashed by U.S. and Israeli strikes on Tehran. The U.S. says Iran has armed, funded and trained the Houthis with help from Hezbollah.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Mojtaba's First Message Makes It Clear: Iran Won't Give An Easy 'Exit' To Trump
content: In Iran, the assassination of a Supreme Leader demands a response, a do-or-die response. Backing down would risk undermining Mojtaba's legitimacy at home and weakening his authority among the hardline institutions that support his regime.

title: PM Modi Unveils Rs 19,680 Crore Projects, Hands Land Pattas To Assam Tea Workers
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday dedicated to the nation and laid the foundation for projects worth around Rs 19,680 crore, with focus on infrastructure and energy sectors.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Amazon Centres To Banks, What Iran's 'Target List' Of Tech Firms Reveals
content: Distributed via Telegram by the IRGC-affiliated Tasnim News Agency, the list named Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, IBM, Oracle, and Palantir - together with their regional offices, data centres, and research facilities, as legitimate targets.

title: "Breaches Bottom Line Of Morality": China Slams Iran School Strike
content: China said on Friday it will donate $200,000 to the parents of students killed in what it called an "indiscriminate" missile strike on a school in Iran early in the Middle East war.

title: Iran Avenges Minab School Massacre With Devastating Data Wipe Attack On US
content: Modern warfare is not limited to how precise a country's aerial strikes, drones and interceptors are. It goes way beyond, and Iran appears to have proven it, in response to the US and Israel's latest onslaught on Tehran and multiple other cities.

title: Are UAE Businesses Impacted By War? What Ajmal Perfumes Chief Said
content: NDTV visited the manufacturing facility of Ajmal Perfumes in Dubai, one of the region's largest perfume brands, to understand how the situation is affecting the market.

title: "Domestic Production Up 30%": Centre Says No Need For LPG Panic Booking
content: LPG Updates: The demand pattern for cooking gas in the country has changed, showing massive panic buying, the government has said, underscoring that domestic production of LPG has gone up by 30 per cent.

title: Trump Says Iran "About To Surrender", 24 Hours Later New Leader Vows Revenge
content: US President Donald Trump told G7 leaders that Iran is "about to surrender" during a virtual meeting on Wednesday, Axios reported.

title: Rs 19 Lakh Crore Lost In Worst Week In 4 Years As Oil Shock Rattles Markets
content: Investors lost about Rs 19 lakh crore as the Indian markets logged their worst week in four years, struggling amid the Middle East conflict and the oil shock from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Dakshineswar Temple Replica Takes Centre Stage At PM Modi's Kolkata Rally
content: For the first time, the BJP is set to drop its traditional backdrop featuring the party symbol and photographs of the Prime Minister, National President, and State President from a political rally stage in West Bengal.

title: 1 Turkish Ship Gets Approval To Cross Hormuz Strait Amid Iran War
content: A Turkish-owned vessel that had been waiting near Iran has been permitted to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after authorities in Tehran approved, Turkey's Transport and Infrastructure Minister Abdulkadir Uraloglu said.

title: US Waiver Frees Up 19 Million Barrels Of Russia Oil For Purchase
content: Russian crude oil and fuel on about 30 tankers in Asian waters is potentially available for purchase after the US granted a temporary waiver to buy cargoes that were already at sea.

title: Opinion: Opinion | What Happens If Saudi Arabia Really 'Asks' Pakistan To Go To War?
content: Since the beginning of the Iran-Israel war, questions have been raised about what role Pakistan would play on the side of the Saudis. The answer is getting murkier.

title: How Trump's Plan For Cuba Would Make US The Island's Patron
content: Speculation about a possible military overthrow of Cuba's Communist regime has swirled around Washington, fueled in part by Senator Lindsey Graham, who told Fox News this week that "Iran going down, and Cuba is next".

title: High Debt, Deficits Dragging Fiscal Health Of These States, See Full List
content: The report evaluates state finances using five broad indicators, including revenue mobilisation, expenditure quality, and fiscal prudence.

title: Stock Market Highlights: Rs 9.5 Lakh Crore Investor Wealth Wiped Out As Oil Shock Rattles Markets
content: Stock Market Highlights: At the close, Nifty was down 488 points at 23,151 while Sensex fell 1,470 points to 74,563.

title: 'Wounded and likely disfigured': How US reacted to Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement
content: US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth claimed Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is wounded and "likely disfigured" following an air strike. Hegseth cited the absence of a voice or video message from Khamenei as evidence of his condition and lack of legitimacy. Meanwhile, Iranian President's son stated Khamenei is "safe and sound."

title: Not Afridi or Akhtar: The last Pakistan star to play an IPL final
content: Sohail Tanvir’s winning run in the 2008 IPL final symbolically marked the last meaningful appearance of Pakistani players in the league. After the 2008 Mumbai attacks, political tensions led to their exclusion from IPL auctions. Though Azhar Mahmood later played as a British citizen, Pakistani players remain effectively barred due to visa issues, political risk, and commercial pressures.

title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp admits using Anthropic's Claude after Pentagon ban
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp confirmed continued use of Anthropic's Claude AI models despite the Pentagon labeling the company a supply chain risk.  The Department of Defense is planning a phase-out, but acknowledges a transition will take time, with potential exemptions for mission-critical operations. This highlights the challenges in disentangling deeply embedded AI systems from defense operations.

title: Watch: Pakistan's Salman Ali Agha run-out sparks controversy during 2nd ODI
content: A controversial run-out of Salman Ali Agha in the second ODI between Bangladesh and Pakistan has ignited social media debate. Agha, believing the ball dead, bent to retrieve it, only for Bangladesh skipper Mehidy Hasan Miraz to swiftly dislodge the bails. Despite Agha's strong knock, his dismissal triggered a Pakistan collapse, impacting the match's outcome.

title: 2 Indians killed in Oman, 10 injured after Iranian drone strikes in Muscat
content: Two Indian nationals have died and 10 others were injured in an attack in Sohar city, Oman. Five of the injured have been discharged from the hospital, while the remaining five are receiving medical treatment. Indian authorities are in contact with Omani officials and are providing assistance to the affected nationals.

title: LPG shortage: What Infosys and HCL Tech's advisory to employees says
content: IT giants Infosys and HCLTech are implementing temporary measures due to an LPG gas shortage impacting India. Infosys has adjusted food court menus and cooking methods across its Bengaluru, Pune, and Chennai campuses. HCLTech has advised its Chennai employees to work from home as cafeteria vendors face operational challenges.

title: ‘Certainly does not help peace’: Zelenskyy on US move to ease Russian oil sanctions
content: Ukraine's President Zelenskyy criticized a US decision to temporarily ease sanctions on Russian oil, warning it could provide Moscow billions for the war and undermine peace efforts. French President Macron echoed concerns, stating Russia is mistaken if it believes the Middle East conflict offers respite. The US claims the waiver aims to stabilize global energy markets.

title: BCCI breaks silence after Sunrisers sign Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed in The Hundred
content: The BCCI has no say in Sunrisers signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed for The Hundred. This is an overseas league. The franchise faces social media criticism. Owner Kavya Maran is targeted. The official X account of Sunrisers Leeds was reportedly suspended. The BCCI cannot intervene in this matter. The franchise has not issued a statement.

title: McKinsey realises AI risk after hackers access 46.5M messages, 728000 sensitive files
content: A cybersecurity researcher's AI agent accessed millions of McKinsey employee chat messages and hundreds of thousands of sensitive file names within two hours, exposing the firm's internal AI platform, Lilli. McKinsey confirmed the vulnerability and patched it promptly, stating that client data was not at risk and no unauthorized third party accessed confidential information.

title: ‘No one can ... ’: Iran hits back at Trump's 'skip FIFA World Cup' remark
content: Iran's national football team has firmly stated that no entity can prevent their participation in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, directly countering US President Donald Trump's safety concerns. The team emphasized that FIFA governs the tournament and their qualification was earned through merit, questioning the host's ability to ensure security if they cannot.

title: 'About to surrender': Trump makes big claim on Iran amid ongoing conflict
content: US President Trump claimed that Iran is "about to surrender" during a G7 call earlier this week, while describing Iran regime as removing a "cancer." Amidst escalating regional strikes, Iran reportedly targeted the USS Abraham Lincoln, though the US denied the incident. CENTCOM confirmed the carrier strike group's ongoing support for operations.

title: Archana Puran Singh proud as son Aryamann nets four vs Pakistan, eyes EPL dream
content: Archana Puran Singh's son, Aryamann Sethi, opened up about his struggles with depression, anxiety, and bullying, which impacted his promising football career. Serious injuries ultimately ended his dream of playing professionally. He also shared experiences of racism and feeling like an outsider due to his parents' fame.

title: Who's next after Surya? IPL 2026 should be an audition for next T20I captain
content: Two years ago, Gautam Gambhir was appointed Team India's coach after he mentored the Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) to the Indian Premier League (IPL) title. With the next T20 extravaganza cycle underway, IPL 2026 should be the audition for India's next T20I captain. The selectors must keep their eyes open because choosing the right man for the captaincy will not be easy.

title: Why did market crash? Nifty50 closes at 23,151, Sensex down over 1,400 points
content: Stock market crash today: Continuing the down trend, Nifty50 and BSE Sensex tanked in trade on Friday as oil prices climbed and the Strait of Hormuz closure halted important global trade. While Nifty50 went below 23,200, BSE Sensex was down over 1,400 points.

title: 'CSK is incomplete without him': Pathan drops big statement on Dhoni's IPL future
content: Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan believes this IPL season could be MS Dhoni's last for Chennai Super Kings.  Pathan emphasized Dhoni's crucial role in team unity and grooming future leaders, even if his playing time is limited.  He suggested CSK aims for a title win to give the legend a fitting farewell.

title: Middle East crisis: 4 crew members killed after US refueling aircraft crashes in Iraq
content: A US KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq on Friday, resulting in the deaths of four of its six crew members. The US military confirmed the incident is under investigation but ruled out hostile or friendly fire. This marks the fourth US military aircraft loss in the Middle East conflict.

title: Thousands of Chinese boats form unusual patterns near Taiwan -what it could mean
content: Thousands of Chinese fishing boats formed unusual geometric patterns in the East China Sea, raising questions about potential military or strategic mobilization. Experts suggest these large-scale, coordinated gatherings, unlike normal fishing, may indicate preparations for crisis scenarios involving Taiwan or Japan.

title: Eid-ul-Fitr 2026: March 20 or 21 in India? Moon sighting to decide date
content: Eid ul-Fitr, the festival of breaking the fast, will be celebrated in India on March 20 or 21, 2026. The exact date depends on the crescent moon sighting. This joyous occasion marks the end of Ramadan. Families will reunite for prayers, feasts, and to exchange greetings. Children will receive Eidi gifts.

title: ‘Border 2’ OTT release: When and where to stream the war drama
content: Sunny Deol’s ‘Border 2’ (Jan 23, 2026 release) earned Rs 392cr+ worldwide. Now on OTT from March 20. Anurag Singh directs 1971 Indo-Pak war saga with Varun Dhawan, Diljit Dosanjh, Ahan Shetty honoring real heroes. Action-packed drama blends battles, sacrifices, and hit track “Ghar Kab Aoge.”

title: The Hundred: 'We had a few players in our list,' says Badani on PAK players
content: Southern Braves coach Hemang Badani detailed the challenges of cricket auctions, emphasising discipline and patience. The team secured a mix of experienced players like Chris Jordan and Marcus Stoinis, alongside promising talent such as Thomas Rew. Badani highlighted David Miller's acquisition as a strategic move for finishing games.

title: How much 18K, 22K and 24K gold costs in your city? Check prices
content: Gold prices saw a significant dip on Friday, with futures trade closing at Rs 1.59 lakh per 10 grams. This decline was influenced by weaker international trends and a stronger US dollar. Analysts highlight the strengthening dollar as a key pressure point, while geopolitical risks and economic signals remain under close observation by traders.

title: $3.5 a shot vs $3m Patriot: How high-energy lasers could replace missiles against Iranian attacks
content: High-energy lasers are emerging as a cost-effective defense against drones and missiles, with costs as low as $3.50 per shot compared to millions for missile interceptors. Despite decades of development and some deployments, challenges remain in scaling the technology for widespread military use due to environmental limitations and production hurdles.

title: Middle East crisis: Jaishankar holds fourth call with Iranian FM; bilateral ties in focus
content: India's External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar held his fourth phone call with Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi, discussing bilateral ties and BRICS issues amidst Middle East tensions. The conversation followed recent discussions on the Strait of Hormuz and maritime security, with Iran blaming US actions for regional instability.

title: Hormuz miscalculation? Trump sideswiped by Iran's closure of Strait
content: Washington underestimated Iran's threat to close the Strait of Hormuz, causing global oil market jitters. Despite planning, economic fallout wasn't fully accounted for, leaving the US scrambling. While officials claim preparedness, limited options and high risks for naval escorts are evident. Iran signals the strait's closure remains a pressure tool.

title: Former Chelsea and Arsenal star Amy Carr dies at 35 after long battle with brain tumour
content: Former England youth international Amy Carr has sadly passed away at the age of 35 after fighting a brain tumour for more than a decade. Carr, who once played for top clubs such as Chelsea F.C. Women, Arsenal W.F.C., and Reading F.C. Women, inspired many people through her courage and determination during her illness. She was first diagnosed with a high-grade brain tumour in 2015.

title: Afghanistan hits Pakistan's Kohat military fort in retaliatory drone strikes
content: Afghanistan retaliated against Pakistani strikes that killed four in Kabul and Kandahar. Afghan forces claimed to have targeted Pakistani military centers in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, including a fortress and command center, following alleged "night-time violations" along the disputed Durand Line. This escalation intensifies existing tensions between the two nations.

title: How LPG issue, oil price surge & Hormuz crisis may hit Indian industry: Explained in 10 points
content: The scarcity of commercial LPG has emerged after shipments from the Gulf region were disrupted amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia. India relies heavily on imports from this region for its LPG requirements.

title: Who is Vanshika Chadha? Meet Kuldeep Yadav’s bride-to-be
content: Indian cricketer Kuldeep Yadav will marry his longtime partner Vanshika Chadha on March 14 in Mussoorie. The couple, childhood friends from Kanpur, saw their friendship grow into a lifelong relationship. Vanshika works at the Life Insurance Corporation of India and maintains a private life away from the spotlight. Wedding festivities begin March 13, with cricket stars like Virat Kohli expected to attend.

title: If Iran pull out of FIFA World Cup, Here's why Iraq and UAE could replace them
content: Iran's participation in the upcoming FIFA World Cup is uncertain due to geopolitical tensions with the United States, a co-host. Citing security and political concerns, Iranian authorities are contemplating withdrawal. If Iran pulls out, FIFA will need to select a replacement, likely from the Asian Football Confederation, with UAE and Iraq being potential candidates.

title: 'Nobody will assign them responsibilities': Why Supreme Court rejected plea on menstrual leave
content: The Supreme Court declined to mandate menstrual leave for women students and employees nationwide, citing concerns about potential negative impacts on women's employment and the risk of reinforcing gender stereotypes. The court emphasized that policy decisions of this nature should be made by government policymakers after consulting stakeholders, rather than through judicial intervention.

title: From cheering low gas cost to taking pride 'when oil prices go up': Trump’s evolving stance
content: President Trump's stance on rising oil prices has shifted, now framing them as beneficial for the US as a major oil producer. This change follows strikes against Iran, which triggered a surge in global oil and petrol prices, impacting the Strait of Hormuz. The administration is grappling with economic consequences and political considerations ahead of midterm elections.

title: This tiny village in Maharashtra has a magical secret that grows inside pumpkins
content: Miraj, a historic city in Maharashtra, is renowned for its musical instrument making. Craftsmen create exquisite sitars and tanpuras using a special type of pumpkin. This tradition, passed down through generations, faces threats from climate change impacting pumpkin size. The craft requires government support for survival. The disappearance of this art would mean losing music, history, and ancestral voices.

title: Kuldeep Yadav wedding: Chahal arrives in Dehradun, says ‘I will dance a lot’
content: Yuzvendra Chahal arrived in Dehradun for Kuldeep Yadav's wedding in Mussoorie, expressing excitement for the festivities. The spinner is set to tie the knot with fiancée Vanshika on March 14 at the Welcomhotel The Savoy Mussoorie. The event is anticipated to be a private yet star-studded affair with several prominent cricketers expected to attend.

title: Tharoor lauds PM Modi's outreach to Iran president, holding talks
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor praised Prime Minister Modi and EAM Jaishankar for engaging with Iran amidst escalating West Asian tensions. He urged a collective push for peace, highlighting the risks to Indian citizens in the Gulf and the nation's role as a constructive voice.

title: Eight weeks of bloodbath: Is Nifty IT facing a pause or the start of a bigger tide?
content: India's IT stocks are facing an eighth consecutive week of losses, wiping out significant market value and sparking debate. While some fear AI's disruptive impact, others see a buying opportunity amid historical rebounds. Foreign investors have exited, but a domestic fund is increasing its IT holdings, betting on a recovery.

title: 'Vital partner in keeping oil prices stable': US envoy lauds India amid Strait of Hormuz crisis
content: US envoy Sergio Gor voiced grave concerns over escalating tensions at the Strait of Hormuz, warning of potential global oil supply disruptions. He highlighted Iran's actions targeting commercial vessels, including those with Indian casualties, and stressed India's crucial role in maintaining stable oil prices.

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu hails AP-Genco’s record single-day power generation
content: Generation of 6,160 MW on March 11 demonstrates the efficiency and dedication of the State’s power generation system, he says

title: Consumers await domestic LPG cylinder delivery, but distributors deny shortage
content: Delays in bookings heighten fears of possible shortage; oil-marketing companies tell government there is no need for apprehension

title: BJP seeks action against LPG agencies for creating artificial shortage in Andhra Pradesh
content: Madhav alleges distributors are delaying deliveries and collecting extra charges; urges State government to conduct inspections and take strict action while assuring that the Centre is working to ensure adequate LPG supply

title: Mysuru Travel Mart to be held in June, Tourism Minister invited
content: The fifth edition of the Mysuru Travel Mart will be held from June 5 to 7 at the Jagannath Centre for Art and Culture in Mysuru.

title: Residents fall ill in Sagar taluk, officers rule out cholera breakout
content: In the last three to four days, many residents of Kargal, Jog, Linganmakki, Aralagodu, Henni, Iduvani, and other villages in the taluk have complained of uneasiness, vomiting, and diarrhoea

title: BITS Amaravati to be the first dedicated AI campus in country
content: Representatives of the institution inform HRD Minister Lokesh that the campus has been designed in line with the vision of the Chief Minister, incorporating next-generation technologies

title: Opposition’s ‘we want LPG’ protest disrupts Lok Sabha
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju slammed the Congress and said the Congress leadership was refusing to understand the pulse of the people

title: Treat women with dignity at police stations, says women’s commission chairperson
content: She also advises women to report to senior officers if they encounter any inappropriate behaviour at police stations.

title: YSRCP leaders pay tributes to poet Molla on birth anniversary
content: They ecall her contribution in making Ramayana accessible to common people through simple Telugu and hail recognition accorded during Y. S. Jagan Mohan Reddy’s tenure

title: Mysuru: CSIR-CFTRI develops mobile procession unit for Northeast
content: The mobile processing unit serves as a viable and profitable business opportunity that can substantially support farmers and producers.

title: Environmentalists seek action against culprits behind felling of trees on banks of Kapila river
content: Environmentalists belonging to Parisarakkagi Naavu urge the government to immediately take action against the culprits responsible for the ‘grave assault on environment’.

title: Take the lead in addressing environmental, nutrition, mental health challenges, women told
content: Educationist Tejaswini Anant Kumar was speaking after inaugurating the women’s day celebrations at Sharnbasva University in Kalaburagi

title: Child protection committees, in-house counsellors, SOPs to report abuse among measures to be implemented in PU colleges
content: During recent visits to PU colleges, KSCPCR finds that necessary safeguard measures are not adequately in place

title: 30 senior Congress MLAs hold dinner meeting to seek Cabinet reshuffle, berths
content: The MLAs said they would visit New Delhi by the end of the month, after the Budget session of the Assembly concludes on March 27, to press their demand before the party leadership

title: Andhra Pradesh gets additional Central assistance of ₹341.48 crore towards cyclone relief
content: The amount is allocated from the National Disaster Response Fund for losses suffered due to Cyclone Montha

title: Aksharam Museum in Kerala all set for expansion
content: Construction for the second, third, and fourth phases of the Aksharam Museum will begin on Saturday. The second phase will include eight galleries marking the evolutionary history of 35 major scripts across the world. The project is funded by ₹16.18 crore from the KIIFB.

title: Minister promises to support gaming and e-sports sector in Andhra Pradesh
content: Interacting with industry representatives, Ramprasad Reddy says the government is keen on exploring policy measures to nurture the sector

title: Manipur group demands action over killing of two Kuki men
content: Kuki CSO Working Committee refuses to claim bodies, seeks NIA probe, relocation of Kuki villages from Ukhrul to Kangpokpi; shutdown called on Imphal–Ukhrul road

title: Tobacco procurement: Piyush Goyal promises steps to ease crisis facing farmers
content: The assurance came during a meeting held at the office of Union Minister for Heavy Industries and Steel H.D. Kumaraswamy in New Delhi on Friday.

title: Airports in Tier-II cities are not financially viable after end of UDAN scheme: Karnataka Minister M.B. Patil
content: Patil said he had proposed to the Union Civil Aviation Minister that the UDAN scheme be extended from three years to five years

title: Make counsellors mandatory in schools to help children who suffered sexual offences: Murty
content: Nominated Rajya Sabha member Sudha Murty said survivors of childhood sexual offences want assurance that "it is not your fault...and that assurance can be given by mother at home, or a teacher or a counsellor."

title: LPG crisis: Bengaluru police launch drive against illegal hoarding and marketing of cylinders
content: According to DCPs, more than 50 warehouses and several goods vehicles were searched in the last two days but nothing suspicious was found.

title: Consignment dropped by Pakistani drone recovered near IB in Jammu
content: Acting on the information, teams of the BSF and Police rushed to the spot, and the consignment was recovered at around 11 a.m. in a forward area following information from locals, officials added.

title: DMK alliance to stage State-wide demonstration against Centre on March 15
content: The parties accuse the Centre  of “withholding funds to Tamil Nadu” under various schemes and failing to take precautionary measures anticipating a shortage of LPG

title: CPI(M) turns the page on party veteran G. Sudhakaran
content: State secretary says it is superfluous to discuss Sudhakaran who has severed links with the party. He says the Ambalappuzha constituency will remain with the LDF since “the vote is for the party, not personalities.”

title: Watch: Explained: What is Euthanasia?
content: India’s Supreme Court has recognised passive euthanasia and the right to die with dignity, but the debate around assisted dying continues across the world. In this video, we explain what euthanasia means, the Harish Rana judgement in India, and the difference between active and passive euthanasia. The video also explores why end-of-life decisions are debated in law and ethics, and how different countries regulate assisted dying.

title: Man enters SSC exam hall in Beed, assaults schoolgirl and flees; parents livid at security breach
content: The incident happened at Doithan centre in Beedsangvi at 10.15 a.m., just before the School Secondary Certificate exam was about to begin, an official said.

title: ‘Aakash’ re-release: When songs powered Puneeth Rajkumar’s blockbuster
content: ‘Aakash’ swapped Puneeth Rajkumar’s tough-guy on-screen image for a charming romantic lead. The songs of the Kannada film, composed by RP Patnaik, is still just as relevant today as it was in 2005

title: New NDA government in Bihar will continue to work under Nitish Kumar's guidance: Samrat
content: The standard of living has improved substantially. Earlier, the State had only about 17 lakh power consumers. Today, the number has crossed 2.17 crore, says Bihar Deputy Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary

title: Infra likely to be focus of Telangana 2026-27 budget as Govt trying to balance welfare and development
content: Major allocations likely to CM’s pet projects like Bharat Future city, Hyderabad Metro Rail expansion

title: India right in condemning Iran’s aggression, but why silent over Khamenei’s assassination: Congress asks govt.
content: In a social media post on X, Congress communications chief Jairam Ramesh said Mr. Modi as well as External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar had not condemned the U.S.-Israel action

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: KPCC tries to persuade K. Sudhakaran to step back from Kannur race; MP’s emotional post sparks debate
content: Sudhakaran’s emotional social media post defending his decision to contest from Kannur triggers political discussion within Congress

title: ‘Love Mocktail 3’ will silence the critics of the franchise’
content: Darling Krishna and Milana Nagaraj, the makers behind the Kannada relationship drama ‘Love Mocktail 3’, open up on the movie.

title: Bill to provide precise definition of ‘transgender’ introduced in Lok Sabha
content: In amendments to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019, the government has proposed to do away with the principle that recognises transgender people’s right to a “self-perceived gender identity”, and has introduced a definition of “transgender persons”

title: Parents of Bekal Beach Fest train-accident victim found dead
content: Preliminary investigation hints at suicide. The couple’s son was run over and killed by a train while he was heading for the beach festival in December 2025

title: Days after launching party, V. Pugazhendi pledges support to DMK
content: After Jayalalithaa’s demise, when the AIADMK faced a leadership crisis, Mr. Pugazhendi threw his weight behind Jayalalithaa’s aide Sasikala and her nephew T.T.V. Dhinakaran.

title: Kochi Corporation in Kerala faces huge biomedical waste backlog as incinerator capacity is reduced
content: One of two incinerators at Common Bio-Medical Waste Treatment Facility of KEIL remained shut for 15 days last month. Though the incinerator is back in operation, KEIL has restricted intake to three tonnes a day, down from five tonnes, as it prioritises hospital waste. One of two incinerators at Brahmapuram developed a snag during trial run

title: Economic Stabilisation Fund to help government respond to global challenges: Sitharaman
content: While replying to the Lok Sabha debate on the Second Supplementary Demand for Grants, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman said that the Centre would meet its fiscal deficit target even with the extra expenditure

title: PM Modi virtually unveils projects worth ₹4,570 crore in Assam's Kokrajhar
content: PM Modi unveiled the projects through video conference from Guwahati after his Kokrajhar visit was cancelled due to heavy rain.

title: Rahul Gandhi spreading misinformation on energy issue, says Giriraj Singh
content: Rahul Gandhi said in the Lok Sabha that the war between the U.S. and Israel, and Iran is going to have far-reaching consequences

title: Jaishankar, Iran’s Araghchi hold 4th phone call since start of West Asian conflict
content: The conversation comes as India ramped up efforts to secure safe passage to the Indian-flagged merchant vessels through the strategic shipping route of Strait of Hormuz that has been partially blocked by Tehran

title: Is an MLA contesting MP poll on another party’s B-Form not defection? asks Harish Rao
content: BRS leader says Khairatabad legislator Danam Nagender was elected as an MLA on the ticket of BRS and openly contested Lok Sabha elections using Congress B-Form

title: Ramzan 2026 : Meet a few chefs who prepare ‘nombu kanji’ at mosques in Thiruvananthapuram
content: Mosques hire proficient hands to prepare the spicy porridge in huge quantities, which devotees have while breaking their fast

title: Justice Lisa Gill assumes charge as AP High Court Judge
content: Justice Lisa Gill has been transferred to the Andhra Pradesh High Court from the Punjab and Haryana High Court, where she had been serving as a Judge

title: T.N. CM Stalin commissions first unit of Udangudi Thermal Power Plant, inaugurates various projects
content: Infrastructural projects, including thermal power plants, roads, schools, worth crores saw inauguration by CM Stalin across Tamil Nadu

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: Kongunadu Makkal Desiya Katchi to contest in two seats on DMK’s ‘Rising Sun’ symbol
content: The seat-sharing agreement was signed between DMK president and Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin and KMDK general secretary E.R. Eswaran at Anna Arivalayam, the DMK headquarters in Chennai

title: Curbs imposed in parts of Kashmir; protests break out in Shia dominated areas
content: The protesters shouted slogans against the U.S. and Israel aggression in Iran, and in support of Palestine

title: Naidu lauds farmer for impressive results through natural farming
content: Rosaiah has cultivated 52 varieties of crops on 75 cents of land by following natural farming practices

title: Cash-for-query case: Supreme Court stays Delhi HC order on sanction for CBI to try Mahua Moitra
content: The cash-for-query case pertains to the allegation that Mahua Moitra asked questions in the Lok Sabha in exchange for cash and gifts from a businessman

title: LPG crisis: Who gets priority in supply of cylinders in Karnataka — hospitals, hostels and residential schools, says Minister
content: Hotels will have to wait for at least a week by which time supply of LPG cylinders are expected to ease: Muniyappa

title: Rajya Sabha election: BJD, Congress ask MLAs not to leave Bhubaneswar till March 16
content: With five candidates in the fray for four Rajya Sabha vacancies, political analysts foresee the possibility of cross-voting, absenteeism, or even intentional rejection of votes

title: LPG shortage forces closure of over 100 Kochi hotels
content: Some eateries that remain open have pared down menus to avoid a complete shutdown, restricting certain items to specific time slots

title: Sasikala announces her party’s name: All India Puratchi Thalaivar Makkal Munnetra Kazhagam
content: Earlier on February 24, 2026 she unveiled the party’s flag at a public meeting in Kamuthi of Ramanathapuram district.

title: INDIA bloc MPs submit notice seeking removal of CEC Gyanesh Kumar in Parliament
content: 193 Parliamentarians, 130 from Lok Sabha and 63 from Rajya Sabha, sign 10-page notice listing seven charges against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar

title: Mandatory booking interval for LPG cylinders is 45 days in rural areas, 25 days in urban areas: Telangana Govt
content: Commercial LPG supply has been temporarily restricted to prioritize essential sectors such as hospitals, educational institutions, old age homes, orphanages and government bodies

title: LPG issue: Lok Sabha proceedings adjourned repeatedly amid Opposition protests
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju slammed Congress leader Rahul Gandhi for refusing to understand the pulse of the people

title: Karnataka’s ₹391 crore plan to dump pollution from Bengaluru on farmers in Kanakapura, get contaminated vegetables in return
content: The report by Mapping Malnad notes that the government is trying to solve a problem by geographical displacement of pollution

title: DRI officials raid illegal Alprazolam manufacturing unit in A.P., seize drugs, worth ₹47 Crore
content: Under ‘Operation White Hammer’, the DRI officials conducted searches on a private chemicals manufacturing factory, which was manufacturing Alprazolam tablets, which comes under psychotropic substance

title: The ACC's most important newcomers and what their teams are saying
content: a wave of new faces reshape the ACC every season . the addition of Malachi Toney via the high school ranks and timely portal additions such as Carson Beck helped springboard the Hurricanes to the college football playoff title game in 2025 .

title: Oppn submits notices seeking motion for CEC’s removal to both House today
content: the notices have been signed by 130 Lok Sabha and 63 Rajya Sabha MPs . it is the first time a notice has been given seeking removal of the CEC . the notice is signed by at least 100 MPs and the required strength is 50 . opposition MPs kept coming till Thursday afternoon to sign the notice .
